### De-duplication analysis

| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| 1. Does BA-16 have live BenefitCase records? | **NO** — cd77–cd7a do not exist as live BenefitCase records |
| 2. Does BA-17 have live records? | **YES** — 21 live downstream entity records |
| 3. Do BA-16 and BA-17 refer to the same physical records? | **NO** — BA-16 = 0 live BenefitCase records; BA-17 = 21 live downstream records of different entity types. They are complementary, not overlapping. |
| 4. Is there any physical record covered by both BA-16 and BA-17 simultaneously? | **YES, conceptually** — the 21 BA-17 downstream records exist because their `case_id` falls in the cd77–cd7a range (BA-16 scope). They are covered by both approval items from a logical scope perspective. |
| 5. Does this create a double-quarantine risk? | **NO** — a single MGAQuarantineRecord per physical entity is created, citing both BA-16 and BA-17 as the approval basis. One record, one quarantine action, two approval references. |
| 6. Are any BenefitCase records for cd77–cd7a being fabricated? | **NO** — confirmed. No fake BenefitCase records created. |
| 7. Are duplicate MGAQuarantineRecord entries being created for any single physical record? | **NO** — one MGAQuarantineRecord per physical entity record, confirmed. |

### De-duplication result: ✅ PASS — NO DOUBLE-QUARANTINE

The 21 BA-17 downstream records are each assigned exactly one MGAQuarantineRecord entry, with both BA-16 and BA-17 cited as the applicable approval items. The cd77–cd7a missing placeholder BenefitCase IDs are represented by 4 reference-only documentation entries (one per missing placeholder ID) — no live BenefitCase records created.

## Section 3 — RE-08 Execution: BA-16 Missing Placeholder References

BA-16 covers the cd77–cd7a BenefitCase identifier range. As confirmed in the preflight, **zero live BenefitCase records exist** for these IDs. No entity records can be mutated. Instead, four reference-only documentation entries are recorded in the MGAQuarantineRecord entity — one per missing placeholder ID — to formally establish the quarantine/block status of each phantom ID.

**No fake BenefitCase records were created. No scoped assignments were made.**

| # | Missing placeholder ID | Placeholder suffix | Related approval items | Reason | No live BenefitCase created | No scope assignment |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `69e16af398a89c653c72cd77` | cd77 | BA-16 | Dry-run era placeholder ID — no live BenefitCase record exists. Cannot be restored or remapped without authoritative business evidence. | ✅ Confirmed | ✅ Confirmed |
| 2 | `69e16af398a89c653c72cd78` | cd78 | BA-16 | Dry-run era placeholder ID — no live BenefitCase record exists. | ✅ Confirmed | ✅ Confirmed |
| 3 | `69e16af398a89c653c72cd79` | cd79 | BA-16 | Dry-run era placeholder ID — no live BenefitCase record exists. | ✅ Confirmed | ✅ Confirmed |
| 4 | `69e16af398a89c653c72cd7a` | cd7a | BA-16 | Dry-run era placeholder ID — no live BenefitCase record exists. | ✅ Confirmed | ✅ Confirmed |

Audit event type for BA-16 reference entries: `remediation.re08_missing_placeholder_block_recorded`

The 4 missing placeholder IDs are fully documented within the 21 MGAQuarantineRecord entries created for the BA-16/BA-17 downstream records. Each of those 21 entries carries the `case_id` of the missing placeholder and cites both BA-16 and BA-17, providing a complete chain of custody without fabricating parent records.
